failingTest: getTagDef('PROSODY') returns undefined instead of the prosody definition
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — add .toLowerCase() in getTagDef comparison
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — add case-insensitivity test block for getTagDef and isTagSupportedByProvider
fixApproach: In getTagDef, lowercase the incoming tagName before comparing against SSML_TAGS entries (which are already lowercase). This is a one-line change. isTagSupportedByProvider inherits the fix automatically since it calls getTagDef. Add a describe block in the test file covering uppercase, mixed-case, lowercase-regression, and unknown-uppercase cases, plus a cross-check on isTagSupportedByProvider.
steps: Modify getTagDef in apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts: normalize tagName with .toLowerCase() before comparing against SSML_TAGS entries

Add a describe('case-insensitive tag lookup') block in apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts covering: uppercase 'PROSODY', mixed-case 'Break', lowercase 'emphasis' (regression), uppercase 'NONEXISTENT' → undefined, and isTagSupportedByProvider('PROSODY', provider) === isTagSupportedByProvider('prosody', provider)
Run npm run test to confirm all existing and new tests pass
summary: Make getTagDef normalization case-insensitive by lowercasing, and add uppercase / mixed-case test coverage.
